description: A plain candid event photograph (wide panorama) with no on-image text or graphic design. It depicts the moment of the oath at an outdoor naturalization ceremony at Pearl Harbor: a row of roughly a dozen people stand with right hands raised taking the Oath of Allegiance, including several in military uniforms (Navy whites, Army, Marine Corps) and civilians, many wearing red-and-white leis. At left an official (back to camera) administers the oath at a microphone while two seated men - one in a National Park Service ranger hat - look on at a draped table. A blue ceremonial flag is at far right; harbor, boats and trees are visible in the background under a partly cloudy sky. Documentary daylight style. Not a designed meme/poster. Tweet celebrates 11 service members becoming new U.S. citizens on Memorial Day at Pearl Harbor.
